subject: AP Microeconomics
classification_standard: A student who has completed ONLY this unit should be able to answer this question.

units.name: Basic Economic Concepts
example_questions: Comparative advantage and terms of trade
Resource allocation in different economic systems

units.name: Supply and Demand
distinguishing_features: Supply and demand model is the CORE
Government intervention in markets (price controls, taxes, subsidies)
boundary_rules: Supply and demand for a specific product = U2
Tariff/quota effects on domestic market = U2
Perfectly competitive market equilibrium (no cost curves) = U2
example_questions: Demand shift due to income change
Tariff effect on domestic price and quantity

units.name: Production, Cost, and the Perfect Competition Model
distinguishing_features: Cost curves (MC, ATC, AVC, AFC) are the core tools
boundary_rules: Perfectly competitive firm profit maximization = U3
Short-run shutdown / long-run exit decisions = U3
IF a question mentions perfect competition AND monopoly → U3 if testing perfect competition, U4 if testing monopoly
example_questions: Draw MC and ATC curves for a perfectly competitive firm
Calculate profit at a given price
Determine whether to shut down in the short run

units.name: Imperfect Competition
distinguishing_features: Imperfectly competitive markets (monopoly, monopolistic competition, oligopoly)
boundary_rules: Comparison of market structures = U4 (if comparing imperfect to perfect competition)

units.name: Factor Markets
distinguishing_features: No product market analysis (unless as context for factor demand)
boundary_rules: MRP = MFC for profit-maximizing hiring = U5
Minimum wage effects in labor market = U5
example_questions: Hiring decision based on MRP and wage
Effect of minimum wage on employment

units.name: Market Failure and the Role of Government
distinguishing_features: Government intervention to correct market failure
boundary_rules: Negative externality and tax/subsidy solution = U6
Public good and free-rider problem = U6
Government regulation to correct market failure = U6
example_questions: Negative externality and socially optimal quantity

cross_unit_rules.name: Highest unit number rule (most important)
cross_unit_rules.rule: If a question involves concepts from multiple units, ALWAYS classify it to the HIGHEST unit number. A student must have learned all involved units to answer this question, so it belongs to the last unit learned. Examples: U2+U3→U3, U3+U4→U4, U2+U5→U5, U1+U3+U6→U6.

cross_unit_rules.name: Product market vs factor market
cross_unit_rules.rule: If the question is about a PRODUCT market (goods/services) → U2, U3, or U4. If the question is about a FACTOR market (labor, land, capital) → U5.

cross_unit_rules.name: Perfect competition vs imperfect competition
cross_unit_rules.rule: If the question tests perfect competition (price taker, MR = P, zero economic profit in long run) → U3. If the question tests monopoly, monopolistic competition, or oligopoly → U4.

cross_unit_rules.name: Market failure vs normal market analysis
cross_unit_rules.rule: If the question asks about socially optimal quantity, externalities, public goods, or government intervention to correct market failure → U6. If the question is about normal supply/demand without market failure → U2.

cross_unit_rules.name: Distractor detection
cross_unit_rules.rule: If a concept from another unit appears ONLY in wrong answer options, it is a DISTRACTOR and does not change the primary unit.

common_pitfalls: NEVER classify by keyword frequency alone
NEVER classify by first keyword mentioned
NEVER let distractors determine the unit
ALWAYS distinguish product market (U2-U4) from factor market (U5)
ALWAYS distinguish normal market analysis (U2-U5) from market failure (U6)
ALWAYS check: would a student who only studied this unit know this concept?
For cross-unit questions: ALWAYS take the HIGHEST unit number, never the core concept unit
Do NOT ignore cross-unit synthesis: any question with U6 concepts belongs to U6, even if it also has U2 concepts
